modules.module_title: Introduction to Arabic Alphabet, Pronunciation & Basic Sounds
Essential Vocabulary: Numbers, Days, Colors & Family Members
Everyday Conversations: Directions, Shopping & Asking Prices
Basic Sentence Construction, Nouns, Verbs & Pronouns
Professional Ethics, Cultural Etiquette & Business Communication

questions.question: Arabic script ko kis direction me padha aur likha jata hai?

options.D: Arabic has zero writing direction limits

questions.explanation: Arabic scripting right-to-left format me start and end hoti hai, horizontal lines me read ki jati hai.

questions.question: Arabic language me standard greeting 'Assalamu Alaikum' ka general correct reply kya hota hai?

options.A: Wa Alaikum Assalam (aur aap par bhi salamati ho)

questions.explanation: Assalamu Alaikum (peace be upon you) ka traditional aur most respectful reply Wa Alaikum Assalam hai.

questions.question: Arabic daily conversation checks me thank you bolne ke liye kaun sa word use kiya jata hai?
questions.explanation: Shukran Arabic me thanks show karne ke liye baseline polite word hai.

questions.question: Arabic numbers checks me, number 'Wahid' kis digit value ko represent karta hai?
questions.explanation: Arabic counting Wahid (1), Ithnan (2), Thalatha (3) coordinate flows se start hoti hai.

questions.question: Arabic vocabulary checks me 'Kitaab' word ka kya meaning hota hai?
questions.explanation: Kitaab common Arabic noun word hai jiska english translation Book hota hai.

questions.question: Arabic grammar checks me gender definitions me masculine structures feminine me convert karne ke liye word ke end me kya lagate hain?

options.A: Ta Marbuta (ة - circular t sound marker)

questions.explanation: Ta Marbuta letter (ة) suffix most masculine nouns ko feminine noun class me convert kar deta hai (eg Jameel to Jameelah).

questions.question: Arabic polite words me request ke liye 'Please' ko kya bola jata hai?
questions.explanation: Min Fadlik requesting statements me 'Please' parameter represent karta hai, polite expressions checks me baseline word.

questions.question: Arabic pronouns checks me first person singular pronoun 'I' (main) ke liye kya word use hota hai?
questions.explanation: Ana represents first-person singular 'I' in both masculine and feminine speech flows.

questions.question: Arabic greetings loops me morning wish 'Sabah Al-Khair' ka standard reply check kya hota hai?
questions.explanation: Sabah Al-Khair (good morning) wish karne par custom reply Sabah An-Noor bola jata hai.

questions.question: Cultural communication ethics checks me, traditional Middle East business meets me handshake rules?

options.A: Handshakes are common and gentle, often followed by placing right hand on heart as mark of respect
options.B: Firm bone crushing grip is mandatory to show power
options.C: Never shake hands at all under any situations

questions.explanation: Right hand over chest post gentle handshakes is traditional Middle Eastern respect acknowledgment gesture.

questions.question: Arabic numbers checks me, number 'Ithnan' represents which numeric value?
questions.explanation: Wahid is 1, Ithnan is 2, Thalatha is 3, Arba'a is 4. Ithnan is 2.

questions.question: Arabic vocabulary checks what does the word 'Bayt' mean?
questions.explanation: Bayt represents house or home. It is a very common Arabic noun.

questions.question: Arabic alphabet checks how many letters make up the Arabic alphabet?
questions.explanation: The Arabic alphabet contains 28 basic letters, all representing consonants, with vowels marked using accents.

questions.question: Arabic greetings what is the meaning of 'Ahlan wa Sahlan'?
questions.explanation: Ahlan wa Sahlan is the standard Arabic welcome greeting for guests and visitors.

questions.question: Arabic travel logs why is learning 'Min Fadlik' (please) critical for retail negotiations?

options.A: Polite indicators soften pricing queries, leading to better vendor discounts
options.B: Required by regional retail tax systems

questions.explanation: Using polite tags like please and thank you displays respect, which is highly valued in Arab retail culture.

questions.question: Arabic grammar 'Definite Article' what is equivalent of 'The' in Arabic?
questions.explanation: 'Al' prefix (eg Bayt to Al-Bayt) shifts indefinite noun to definite, matching 'The' in English.

questions.question: Everyday greetings 'Good evening' what is standard phrase in Arabic?
questions.explanation: Masa'a Al-Khair is standard evening wish greeting, whereas Sabah Al-Khair is morning wish.

questions.question: Arabic vocabulary checks 'Colors' what is color 'Ahmad' or 'Ahmar' (red)?
questions.explanation: Ahmar represents red, Azraq represents blue, Akhdar represents green, and Aswad represents black.

questions.question: Arabic dialogue prompts 'Asking how are you' (to masculine listener)?
questions.explanation: Kaifa Haluk is standard phrase to query listener well-being status, answered by 'Alhamdulillah'.

questions.question: Arabic business verifications 'Halal compliance labels' what does Halal mean?

options.A: Permissible / Lawful according to Islamic guidelines

questions.explanation: Halal denotes lawful guidelines, critical parameter for food/beverage and cosmetic trade certifications.

questions.question: Arabic phonetics 'Sun letters vs Moon letters' how does 'Alif-Lam' pronunciation shift in Sun letters?

options.A: 'L' sound is silent and assimilates into next consonant (eg 'Al-Shams' is pronounced 'Ash-Shams')
options.C: Sun letters make the prefix Al turn into red text

questions.explanation: Sun letters (like Sh, S, T, N) absorb the 'L' sound of 'Al' completely, leading to consonant doubling.

questions.question: Arabic daily expressions 'Congratulations' what phrase is spoken?
questions.explanation: Mabrook is the standard colloquial Arabic expression used to congratulate someone on successes.

questions.question: Arabic vocabulary checks 'Family' what is the word for 'Father'?
questions.explanation: Ab represents Father, Umm represents Mother, Akh represents Brother, and Ukht represents Sister.

questions.question: Arabic dialogue prompts 'Asking for prices' how to query price in Arab markets?
questions.explanation: 'Bikam' means how much, 'hadha' means this. Combined, they query costs at local retail shops.

questions.question: Arabic business etiquette 'Meeting timings' how are business schedules respected?

options.A: Arrive on time, but be prepared for flexible meetings as business relationships are prioritized over rigid clocks
options.B: Meetings are cancelled if late by 1 minute
options.C: Work details are discussed strictly by email without meetings

questions.explanation: Building trust and rapport takes precedence in Arab culture, which often leads to longer, more flexible meetings.

questions.question: Arabic grammar 'Short Vowels' (Harakat) what is 'Dammah' stroke sound?

options.A: Short 'u' sound (looks like mini comma above letters)
options.B: Short 'a' sound (looks like flat slash above)

questions.explanation: Fatha is 'a', Kasrah is 'i', Dammah is 'u'. Harakat guide word pronunciation in Arabic.

questions.question: Arabic polite words 'You are welcome' what is standard reply to Shukran?
questions.explanation: Afwan is the polite standard reply to Shukran, and also serves as pardon/excuse me.

questions.question: Arabic vocabulary checks 'Days of the week' what is 'Yom Al-Ahad' (Sunday)?
questions.explanation: Yom Al-Ahad is Sunday. Weekend rest days differ across Arab countries, but Ahad marks the start of the week.

questions.question: Arabic travel phrases 'Asking where' how to say 'Where is the hotel?'?
questions.explanation: 'Ayna' means where, 'al-funduq' means hotel. Combined, they query hotel locations.

questions.question: Arabic grammar 'Possessive Pronouns' how to say 'My book' in Arabic (using Kitaab)?
questions.explanation: Adding 'ee' suffix denotes first-person singular possessive 'My', turning Kitaab (book) into Kitaabee (my book).

questions.question: Arabic vocabulary checks 'Numbers' what is number 'Khamsah' value?
questions.explanation: Wahid(1), Ithnan(2), Thalatha(3), Arba'a(4), Khamsah(5). Khamsah is 5.

questions.question: Arabic daily chats 'Leave-taking' what is standard goodbye phrase?
questions.explanation: Ma'as Salama is the standard polite goodbye phrase, wishing safe travels/stays.

questions.question: Arabic grammar 'Nouns gender' what category does word 'Sayyara' (car) belong to?
questions.explanation: Sayyara ends with Ta Marbuta (ة), marking it grammatically as a feminine noun.

questions.explanation: Friday is the holy congregational prayer day and is a weekend holiday across most Muslim-majority countries.

questions.question: Arabic phonetics 'Sun letter vs Moon letter' what defines a Moon letter?

options.A: 'L' sound in Alif-Lam prefix is clearly pronounced (eg 'Al-Qamar' remains 'Al-Qamar')
options.C: Moon letters are written in gold ink

questions.explanation: For Moon letters (like Q, K, B, M, A), the prefix 'Al' is pronounced clearly, unlike the assimilation seen with Sun letters.
